MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Feb-27-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Yosef Peretz, Esq. of PERETZ & ASSOCIATES, T:415.732.3777 ext.102, for defendant/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff's objection having been withdrawn, and there being no other appearance, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT DYLAN TONG's MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS as follows:

Defendant Dylan K.Y. Tong's motion to stay this action for all purposes until the final resolution and disposition of the related action currently in trial, Jonathan Bornstein v. Daniel Bornstein, et al., Case No. RG16-830541 (Alameda Ct. Super. Ct.) is granted. (See Code Civ. Proc. 526(a)(6) [injunction may be granted "[w]here the restraint is necessary to prevent a multiplicity of judicial proceedings"]; Freiberg v. City of Mission Viejo (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1484, 1489 ["Trial courts generally have the inherent power to stay proceedings in the interests of justice and to promote judicial efficiency."].)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
